title: NOURA daily-greens DTC paid-social video ad package: UGC talking-head mashup cut with multi-hook variants, 9:16 / 1:1 / 16:9 reframes, cleaned UGC audio, on-brand caption / callout graphic plates, and a graded scroll-stopper thumbnail

source.reference: Video Ads for DTC Supplement Brand (UGC / VSL / Mashup)

grounding_note: GROUNDED in the real PeoplePerHour brief 'Video Ads for DTC Supplement Brand (UGC / VSL / Mashup)': a direct-to-consumer supplement brand supplies a batch of raw user-generated (UGC) talking-head creator clips plus product b-roll and wants them cut into several short paid-social ad variants that test DIFFERENT HOOKS; each ad reframed to the platform native ratios (9:16 for TikTok / Reels / Shorts, 1:1 for feed, 16:9 for YouTube / VSL); on-brand text, captions and callout graphics added; the raw UGC audio cleaned up; and platform-ready cuts plus a scroll-stopping thumbnail delivered; conversion-focused, fast turnaround, lean budget. REASONABLY ENRICHED for a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark and tagged honestly with a FICTIONAL, IP-clean brand — NOURA, a DTC daily-greens + adaptogen powder (no real trademark, no real product/health data): the open 'raw UGC + b-roll' bundle is fixed to concrete inputs (three vertical talking-head creator clips, one product b-roll clip, a noisy-room UGC audio situation, a flat wordmark PNG, a photoreal creator thumbnail still, a brand color-reference frame, a hooks/angles CSV, a caption-cue JSON, and a brand-kit note with a health-claims compliance line); the ad variants and reframes are produced by the [A] async Quick Cut / reframe tools; and because NO Adobe connector tool composites motion graphics, captions, or text onto video frames, the brief's 'on-brand text / captions / callout graphics' are delivered as (a) a supplied caption-cue DATA sheet and (b) standalone on-brand graphic PLATES (an opaque lower-third caption bar, an opaque callout badge, a vector logo SVG, and a graded end-card) the editor lays onto the timeline — exactly the reusable overlay assets a UGC ad editor drops in during the manual finish. The multi-hook variants are SEPARATE Quick Cut passes (distinct target_duration + user_prompt over the same footage), NOT exact-timestamp trims. video_resize is a same-length reframe (aspect only, no trimming). media_enhance_speech is voice-only UGC-audio cleanup. No generative fill, text-to-image, from-scratch design, upscaling, object removal, or on-video compositing is claimed; image_fill_area is solid-color only; every step maps to a real Adobe connector tool.
one_line_ask: Turn NOURA's batch of raw UGC creator talking-head clips plus product b-roll, a flat wordmark, a thumbnail hero still, a brand color reference, and a hooks/captions brief into a paid-social ad package: an AI mashup master cut plus two tighter hook-variant reels, each reframed to 9:16 / 1:1 / 16:9 (same length), cleaned UGC audio, on-brand lower-third and callout graphic plates plus a vector logo, a fully graded scroll-stopper thumbnail, and a caption cue sheet — all copied into one handoff folder with a client share link.
full_brief:
NOURA is a direct-to-consumer wellness brand — a daily greens + adaptogen powder that (in our words) 'actually tastes like something you'd drink on purpose.' We run paid social on TikTok, Instagram Reels, Meta feed and YouTube, and we're scaling our UGC ad program. For this assignment we're handing over a batch of raw creator footage and want it cut into a short-form ad package we can put straight into ad sets and A/B test.

What we're sending: (a) three raw vertical UGC talking-head clips from three different creators — a kitchen 'morning routine' take, a bathroom-mirror 'get ready with me' take, and a post-workout porch take — all shot on phones, ungraded, with real room audio (echo, clatter, HVAC) that needs cleaning; (b) one product b-roll clip — the powder being scooped into water, stirred, condensation on the glass, the tub and a single-serve sachet on a marble counter; (c) our wordmark logo as a flat PNG; (d) a photoreal hero still of a creator holding the tub, straight off the phone (flat, dull white balance), to become the ad thumbnail / cover; (e) a brand color-reference frame representing the bright, sunny, fresh-green look we grade toward; (f) a hooks CSV listing the different opening hooks / angles / CTAs we want to test; (g) a caption-cue JSON for the on-screen captions; and (h) a brand-kit note with our colors, fonts, voice, and a compliance line (structure/function language only — NO disease or medical claims on any asset).

Deliverables we need, mapped to Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools with every step honestly tagged for execution mode:

A. AUDIO — clean the raw UGC talking-head audio so the soundbites are usable: denoise / de-room the creator narration (voice-only; not music mastering).

B. VIDEO — (1) probe the raw clips so we know resolution / frame rate / duration / which have usable audio before cutting; (2) summarize the footage + b-roll so we agree on the strongest soundbites and a logical flow, steered by our hooks CSV; (3) produce the primary deliverable — one polished ~35s UGC MASHUP master that weaves all three creators + b-roll around Hook A; (4) produce two tighter hook-variant reels as their own passes — an ~18s Hook B 'skeptic-turned-believer' cut and a ~15s Hook C 'results' cut (repurposed from the same footage, NOT exact-timestamp trims); (5) reframe: the master to 16:9 (YouTube / VSL) and 1:1 (feed), and each hook-variant reel to 9:16 (TikTok / Reels / Shorts) — same length, no trimming.

C. THUMBNAIL / COVER — from the flat hero still, build a scroll-stopping graded 9:16 cover: auto-tone baseline, push the bright warm brand color temperature toward our reference, shape the fresh-green + warm-skin HSL, select and pop the creator/product subject, lift the highlights for the airy look, and crop clean to 1080x1920.

D. ON-BRAND TEXT / GRAPHIC PLATES (standalone PNG / SVG references, NOT composited onto the video) — recommend an on-brand caption typeface; vectorize the wordmark to a clean SVG for the end-card and corner bug; build an opaque lower-third caption-bar plate and an opaque callout-badge plate by selecting a region on the graded canvas and solid-filling it with brand color (position/color references the editor rebuilds on the timeline); and a graded end-card the ads close on. Captions themselves ship as the supplied caption-cue sheet.

E. PACKAGING — copy the cleaned audio, all the cuts + reframes, the thumbnail, the end-card, the logo SVG, the plates, and the caption sheet into one dated handoff folder and give us a share link.

Heads-up we already understand: frame-accurate timeline cuts, animated motion graphics, burned-in captions/text, hard transitions, and beat-synced music are a manual Premiere / After Effects finish and are OUT of scope for the automated connector pass. The automated pass delivers the AI mashup + hook variants, the reframes, the UGC audio cleanup, the graded thumbnail and end-card, the vector logo, the caption/callout plates, and the caption sheet — the human editor does the final timeline polish, burns the captions/callouts using the plates, and locks the logo on top. Fast turnaround, lean budget — efficient workflow over flashy effects.

inputs.name: raw_ugc_creator_kitchen.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Handheld vertical 9:16 phone selfie footage, 1080x1920, ~15 seconds, a woman in her early 30s in a bright sunlit modern kitchen holding a matte kraft tub of green 'NOURA' daily-greens powder up toward the lens, talking casually and enthusiastically to camera as if filming a morning-routine UGC ad, natural hand gestures, unscripted cadence, she glances at the tub then back to camera; flat ungraded phone-camera look, slightly overexposed window behind her, real skin texture and flyaway hair, mild handheld sway, authentic native audio of her voice with kitchen room echo and faint fridge/HVAC hum and occasional counter clatter, no captions or text overlays, not glossy or cinematic
inputs.realism_notes: Genuine UGC talking-head look: flat exposure, real handheld motion, unscripted delivery, native ROOMY audio (echo + HVAC + clatter) so media_enhance_speech has a real voice-cleanup job and media_summarize has real dialogue to summarize. Clear spoken soundbite so it can anchor a hook. Ungraded so it reads as raw creator footage.

inputs.name: raw_ugc_creator_bathroom.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Handheld vertical 9:16 phone footage, 1080x1920, ~14 seconds, a man in his late 20s filming a bathroom-mirror 'get ready with me' selfie in the morning, holding a single-serve NOURA sachet, tearing it open and tipping it into a glass of water on the counter while talking to the mirror/camera in a relaxed skeptical-but-won-over tone; mixed warm bathroom bulb light and cool window light, flat ungraded phone look, real mirror reflection and slight lens smudge, authentic native audio of his voice with hard tiled-room reverb and a running-tap moment, no text overlays, candid not cinematic
inputs.realism_notes: Second creator, different setting and a harder reverberant tiled-room acoustic plus a tap-water moment, so the audio-cleanup and the summarize step have varied, genuinely-noisy dialogue. Mixed color temperature gives the grade reference a believable contrast to the bright kitchen clip.

inputs.name: raw_ugc_creator_porch.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Handheld vertical 9:16 phone footage, 1080x1920, ~12 seconds, a woman in athletic wear sitting on a sunny porch just after a workout, slightly out of breath, holding a shaker bottle of mixed green NOURA drink and talking energetically to camera about how it fits her post-gym routine, taking a sip mid-sentence; bright natural daylight, flat ungraded phone-camera color, real sweat sheen and stray hair, gentle handheld drift, authentic native audio of her voice with outdoor ambience (light breeze, distant birds/traffic) and slight wind-on-mic, no text overlays, documentary realism not a polished commercial
inputs.realism_notes: Third creator for a true multi-creator MASHUP; outdoor wind-on-mic and ambience is a distinct audio-repair challenge and a distinct visual environment so the quick_cut engine has three visually and tonally different talking heads to weave. Sip action is a natural product-in-use beat.

inputs.name: product_broll_mixing.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Vertical 9:16 studio product b-roll, 1080x1920, ~10 seconds, macro tabletop shots of NOURA daily-greens: a spoon scooping bright matcha-green powder from an open kraft tub, the powder tipping into a tall glass of water, a spoon stirring it into a swirl, condensation beading on the cold glass, then a clean hero arrangement of the tub and a single-serve sachet on a pale marble counter with a sprig of mint and soft window light; shallow depth of field, gentle slow push-ins, slightly flat ungraded color straight off the camera, crisp real product textures, only ambient studio room tone (no dialogue), no text overlays
inputs.realism_notes: No-dialogue product b-roll to intercut with the three talking heads — gives the mashup its product-in-use beauty beats and gives the summarize/quick_cut steps distinct high-engagement visual moments (pour, stir, condensation). Ungraded/flat so it matches the raw-footage look the edit shapes.

inputs.name: thumbnail_hero_still.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal vertical still, 1080x1920 portrait, a smiling woman in her early 30s in a bright modern kitchen holding a matte kraft tub of NOURA green powder toward the camera at chest height, looking straight at the lens with a warm candid expression, ample clean space above her head; deliberately flat and slightly dull straight-off-the-phone color with a slightly cool/greenish white-balance cast and soft low contrast, real skin pores and individual flyaway hairs, natural window light, photoreal documentary snapshot, no text, no logo, no graphic overlays
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately flat, low-contrast and slightly-off white balance so image_apply_auto_tone, image_adjust_color_temperature, image_adjust_hsl and image_adjust_light_portions all have real corrective work toward the bright warm brand look. A single clear human+product subject so image_select_by_prompt yields a meaningful subject-pop mask. Clean headroom so the crop to a scroll-stopper cover works. Photoreal => gpt-image-2.

inputs.name: noura_wordmark_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat minimalist brand logo on a transparent background: a small simple leaf/sprout mark to the left of the wordmark 'NOURA' set in a clean rounded geometric sans-serif, single solid fresh-green brand color (#3FA45C), crisp clean edges, high contrast, no gradients, no photographic texture, no drop shadow, centered, 2200x700 PNG with alpha
inputs.realism_notes: Flat solid-color, crisp-edged art with transparent alpha so image_vectorize produces a clean scalable SVG and the mark composites as a corner/end-card lockup. Text-bearing => gemini-3-pro-image.

inputs.name: brand_color_reference.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A bright, airy, sunny wellness brand color-reference photograph: a clean flatlay on pale cream marble of a NOURA green-powder tub, a glass of vivid matcha-green drink, fresh mint leaves and lemon slices, warm morning sunlight with soft natural shadows, fresh green + warm cream palette, high-key exposure, crisp and appetizing. Represents the bright, fresh, warm look the whole ad package should be graded toward.
inputs.realism_notes: Provides a believable bright/warm/fresh-green grade target for the color-temperature and HSL grade steps on the thumbnail; photoreal => gpt-image-2. Used as a match reference for the grade, not itself a deliverable.

inputs.name: ad_brief_hooks.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns variant,duration_target_s,hook_line,angle,priority_soundbite,cta holding the paid-social ad hook plan for NOURA daily greens, 3 priority-ordered rows: A,35,'The greens that don''t actually taste like grass','UGC mashup / social proof — three real people, one honest reaction','It doesn''t taste like a lawn, I promise','Get 20% off your first tub — code GLOW20'; B,18,'I was SO skeptical about greens powders...','skeptic-turned-believer VSL angle','I did not expect to actually crave this','Try it risk-free — link in bio'; C,15,'3 scoops in, here''s what changed','results / routine angle','My afternoon slump just... stopped','Start your morning right — code GLOW20'. Keep all language structure/function only, no disease or medical claims.
inputs.realism_notes: Reads like a growth marketer's hook-test brief; passed as free-text steering into media_summarize and the three Quick Cut passes so each variant surfaces its hook + soundbite — NOT as exact-timestamp cut instructions. Health-claims-safe copy matches the brand-kit compliance line.

inputs.name: caption_cues.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a JSON object keyed by variant (A, B, C), each a small array of on-screen caption cues, each cue {index, start_seconds, end_seconds, text}, short punchy all-caps-style lines under 30 characters matching a bright, upbeat UGC supplement ad (e.g. 'GREENS THAT SLAP', 'NO GRASS AFTERTASTE', 'ONE SCOOP. THAT''S IT.', '20% OFF: GLOW20'); 5-6 cues for variant A, 4 for B, 3 for C, all structure/function language only, no disease or medical claims.
inputs.realism_notes: Realistic per-variant caption cue sheet the editor burns onto the reels during the manual finish; gives a concrete captions deliverable as a DATA asset without claiming the connector burns text onto video frames.

inputs.name: brand_kit.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: Write a short brand-kit note for NOURA daily greens: brand colors as hex (fresh green #3FA45C, warm cream #F5EEDD, deep forest accent #1E3A2B, sunny highlight #F4C63D); typography intent (a friendly rounded geometric sans for headlines/captions, a clean humanist sans for body); brand voice (warm, honest, a little cheeky, never clinical); lower-third and callout usage notes (green bar with cream text for captions, cream badge with forest text for fact callouts); and a compliance line: 'All ad copy uses structure/function language only — no disease, cure, treatment, or medical claims, per DSHEA-style supplement advertising rules.'
inputs.realism_notes: Gives font_recommend and the plate/color choices a concrete brand system to honor, and encodes a realistic health_wellness compliance constraint (no disease/medical claims) that steers the copy across the package. Passed as free-text steering, not as machine instructions.

outputs.name: noura_ugc_clean.wav
outputs.spec: Cleaned UGC talking-head audio, voice-only: creator narration denoised / de-roomed from kitchen echo + HVAC, tiled-bathroom reverb, and porch wind/ambience via media_enhance_speech (voice enhancement, not music/loudness mastering). [A] async product action — retrieval is entitlement/widget-polled. Intended to sit under the mashup cuts during the manual timeline finish.

outputs.name: noura_master_16x9.mp4
outputs.spec: Primary deliverable: polished ~35s UGC MASHUP master (Hook A), an AI Quick Cut weaving the three creator talking heads + product b-roll around the top hook/soundbite, then reframed to 16:9 1920x1080 (H.264 target on export) for YouTube / VSL placements. Runtime is a best-effort target (the Quick Cut assembly + reframe are [A] async product actions whose exact final duration/spec are entitlement/widget-polled, not fully headless-retrievable). Frame-accurate cuts, burned-in captions, animated motion graphics, hard transitions, and beat-synced music are the flagged manual Premiere finish and are NOT part of this automated deliverable.

outputs.name: noura_master_1x1.mp4
outputs.spec: 1:1 square (1080x1080) reframe of the Hook A mashup master, SAME length (no trimming), for Meta / Instagram feed placements. Produced by video_resize of the master cut. [A] async.

outputs.name: noura_reel_9x16_hookB.mp4
outputs.spec: 9:16 vertical reel (1080x1920) of the ~18s Hook B 'skeptic-turned-believer' variant — a separate tighter Quick Cut pass over the same footage (repurposed, NOT an exact-timestamp trim of the master), reframed to vertical, SAME length. For TikTok / Reels / Shorts. [A] async.

outputs.name: noura_reel_9x16_hookC.mp4
outputs.spec: 9:16 vertical reel (1080x1920) of the ~15s Hook C 'results / routine' variant — the tightest separate Quick Cut pass, reframed to vertical, SAME length. For TikTok / Reels / Shorts. [A] async.

outputs.name: noura_thumbnail_1080x1920.png
outputs.spec: Scroll-stopping graded 9:16 cover / thumbnail, PNG 1080x1920: auto-toned, warm/bright color-temperature-graded toward the brand reference, fresh-green + warm-skin HSL shaped, subject-popped (creator + product), highlight-lifted for the airy look, then subject-aware cropped/resized to exactly 1080x1920 from the hero still (a straight reframe of the existing frame — no canvas extension or padding). Built to carry the NOURA wordmark as a corner lockup; the actual logo placement/compositing is the flagged human-finish step (the connector chain produces this graded cover without the logo burned in).

outputs.name: noura_endcard_1080x1920.jpg
outputs.spec: On-brand end-card the ads close on: the graded thumbnail cover with a subtle NOURA green/cream brand color overlay applied (image_apply_color_overlay), 1080x1920, leaving space for the logo lockup and CTA the editor adds during the manual finish.

outputs.name: noura_wordmark_logo.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized SVG of the flat NOURA wordmark + leaf mark, for scalable end-card and corner-bug use. Produced by image_vectorize tracing the supplied flat PNG.

outputs.name: lower_third_caption_bar.png
outputs.spec: Opaque lower-third caption-bar plate frame (1080x1920 PNG): a horizontal region of the graded end-card canvas solid-filled with brand green (#3FA45C) to give the editor an exact on-brand caption-bar swatch/position reference to drop on the timeline (solid-color fill only, no text composited; NOT a transparent cutout — headless selection-fill paints a region onto the existing opaque canvas). The on-brand caption typeface is delivered as a font recommendation, not burned into the plate.

outputs.name: callout_badge_plate.png
outputs.spec: Opaque callout-badge plate frame (1080x1920 PNG): a badge-shaped region of the same end-card canvas solid-filled with warm cream (#F5EEDD) as an on-brand fact-callout swatch/position reference, same selection-fill technique (solid-color fill only; NOT a transparent cutout).

outputs.name: noura_caption_cues.json
outputs.spec: On-screen caption cue sheet: the supplied per-variant caption cues ({index, start_seconds, end_seconds, text}) finalized into the handoff folder as the on-screen text asset the editor burns onto the reels during the manual finish — the connector does not composite text onto video frames.

outputs.name: noura_ad_handoff_folder
outputs.spec: One dated Creative Cloud handoff folder gathering the cleaned UGC audio, all cuts + reframes (master 16:9, square 1:1, two 9:16 hook reels), the graded thumbnail, the end-card, the logo SVG, both plates, and the caption cue sheet, plus a client share link — the organized editable-asset set for the Premiere handoff.

connector_workflow.note: Create a managed Creative Cloud project folder ('NOURA_Paid_Social_Ads') to hold every source + produced asset. Root of the deliverable package. [C].

Ingest the client-supplied source files into project_folder — the three raw UGC clips (kitchen, bathroom, porch), the product b-roll, the flat wordmark PNG, the thumbnail hero still, the brand color reference, the hooks CSV, the caption-cue JSON, and the brand-kit note. Returns stored CC asset handles for every source file; the chain's root inputs now live in the project. [C].

Probe the four raw video assets (three UGC clips + b-roll) for resolution, frame rate, duration, and audio-track presence so we confirm all clips are usable and know which have usable dialogue before cutting. Read-only metadata probe. Consumes the ingested video assets. [C].

Clean the raw UGC talking-head audio (voice-only): denoise / de-room the creator narration from kitchen echo + HVAC, tiled-bathroom reverb, and porch wind/ambience so the soundbites are usable. Consumes the ingested UGC clips' audio. [A] async-widget (voice enhancement, not music/loudness mastering).

Summarize the three UGC talking heads + product b-roll (content / strongest soundbites / logical flow) so we agree on the beats before cutting, steered by the ad_brief_hooks.csv priorities and informed by which clips footage_metadata (step 3) flagged as having usable audio. Consumes the ingested video assets. [A] async-widget (content/flow summary, not beat/tempo analysis).

Recommend an on-brand caption / lower-third typeface honoring the brand-kit note's 'friendly rounded geometric sans' intent, to guide the caption burn-in and the plate styling. Advisory recommendation only (no letterform design, no text composited). Consumes the ingested brand-kit note. [C].

Primary deliverable base: AI Quick Cut MASHUP of the three creator talking heads + product b-roll into a polished ~35s master around Hook A ('the greens that don't taste like grass'), prompted by the agreed soundbites from step 5 + the hooks CSV. Visual-engagement AI assembly. [A] async-widget. Frame-accurate cuts / transitions / motion graphics / beat-sync remain the flagged manual finish.

Produce the ~18s Hook B 'skeptic-turned-believer' variant as its OWN tighter Quick Cut pass over the same footage (repurposed, NOT an exact-timestamp trim of the master), prompt-focused on the skeptic soundbite + the bathroom-mixing beat. [A] async-widget.
Produce the ~15s Hook C 'results / routine' variant as the tightest separate Quick Cut pass, prompt-focused on the porch post-workout soundbite + a product beauty beat. [A] async-widget.
Reframe the Hook A mashup master (step 7) to 16:9 1920x1080 for YouTube / VSL — SAME length, no trimming. [A] async-widget.
Reframe the same Hook A mashup master (step 7) to 1:1 square 1080x1080 for feed — SAME length, no trimming. [A] async-widget.
Reframe the Hook B variant (step 8) to 9:16 vertical 1080x1920 for TikTok / Reels / Shorts — SAME length, no trimming. [A] async-widget.
Reframe the Hook C variant (step 9) to 9:16 vertical 1080x1920 for TikTok / Reels / Shorts — SAME length, no trimming. [A] async-widget.
Lay down an auto-tone baseline on the flat, low-contrast thumbnail hero still — first step of the scroll-stopper grade chain. Consumes the ingested hero still. [C].
Correct the slightly cool/greenish phone white balance and push toward the bright warm brand look, matching the ingested brand_color_reference frame. Consumes step-14 + the color reference. [C].
Targeted HSL: bring the fresh matcha-green of the tub/product forward and keep warm natural skin tones, on-brand. Consumes step-15. [C].
Auto-select the creator holding the NOURA tub to drive a selective subject pop. Consumes step-16 and returns a maskURI. [C].
Apply a vibrance/saturation lift only within the subject mask from step 17 (subject pop) on the graded image from step 16, so the creator + product separate from the bright background. [C].
Lift the highlights / lighter portions for the bright, airy high-key scroll-stopper look on-brand. Consumes step-18. [C].

Subject-aware crop/resize to exactly 1080x1920 (9:16) for the cover / thumbnail, keeping the creator + product on a rule-of-thirds line with clean headroom for a caption (a straight reframe of the existing frame — no canvas extension or padding). Consumes step-19 -> thumbnail deliverable. [C].

Vectorize the flat NOURA wordmark + leaf mark PNG into a clean scalable SVG for the end-card and corner bug. Consumes the ingested logo. The thumbnail/end-card + logo composite lockup is the flagged human-finish placement step, since the connector does not composite. [C].

Apply a subtle NOURA green/cream brand color overlay to the graded thumbnail cover (step 20) to produce the on-brand end-card the ads close on, leaving room for the logo lockup + CTA the editor adds later. [C].
On the end-card canvas (step 22) reused as the opaque plate canvas, select a horizontal lower-third caption-bar region. Consumes step-22. [C].

Solid-fill the selected region with brand green (#3FA45C) to produce the lower-third caption-bar plate — an opaque 1080x1920 swatch/position reference (solid-color fill only, no text composited; NOT a transparent cutout, since headless selection-fill paints onto the existing opaque canvas). The recommended caption font from step 6 guides the editor's later burn-in. Consumes step-23. [C].
On the same plate canvas, select a rounded badge-shaped region for the on-screen fact callout. Consumes step-24. [C].

Solid-fill the badge region with warm cream (#F5EEDD) to produce the callout-badge plate — an opaque swatch/position reference (solid-color fill only; NOT a transparent cutout). Consumes step-25. [C].

connector_workflow.inputs_from: noura_ugc_clean.wav, noura_master_16x9.mp4, noura_master_1x1.mp4, noura_reel_9x16_hookB.mp4, noura_reel_9x16_hookC.mp4, noura_thumbnail_1080x1920.png, noura_endcard_1080x1920.jpg, noura_wordmark_logo.svg, lower_third_caption_bar.png, callout_badge_plate.png, ingested_assets
connector_workflow.note: Copy every produced deliverable — cleaned UGC audio (4), master 16:9 (10) + square 1:1 (11), the two 9:16 hook reels (12,13), the graded thumbnail (20), end-card (22), logo SVG (21), both plates (24,26) — plus the supplied caption-cue sheet (noura_caption_cues.json, from ingested_assets) into one dated handoff folder in the client's Creative Cloud so source + deliverables sit together for easy tweaks. [C].
Generate a client share link to the handoff folder (step 27) so NOURA can preview and download the whole ad package in one place. [C].

chaining_note: Strict output->input chain. Steps 1-2 stand up the project: create folder -> asset_add_file yields ingested_assets (every source file). video_metadata(3) probes the four raw clips; media_enhance_speech(4) consumes the ingested UGC audio -> cleaned track; media_summarize(5) consumes the ingested recordings + b-roll (informed by footage_metadata) -> footage_summary, which then steers three independent Quick Cut passes: the ~35s Hook A mashup master(7), the ~18s Hook B variant(8), and the ~15s Hook C variant(9), each consuming ingested_assets + footage_summary as free-text hook/soundbite steering (repurposed passes, NOT exact-timestamp trims); font_recommend(6) consumes the ingested brand-kit note to advise the caption typeface. Four video_resize reframes then each consume a specific cut: master(7)->16:9(10) and ->1:1(11); Hook B(8)->9:16(12); Hook C(9)->9:16(13) — all same-length. In parallel a 7-link headless grade chain threads the flat hero still: apply_auto_tone(14)->adjust_color_temperature(15, also reading the ingested brand color reference as match target)->adjust_hsl(16)->select_by_prompt(17) makes a subject mask that, with the graded image(16), drives masked adjust_vibrance_and_saturation(18) subject-pop->adjust_light_portions(19)->crop_and_resize(20)=graded thumbnail; image_vectorize(21) consumes the ingested logo -> logo SVG; apply_color_overlay(22) consumes the graded thumbnail(20) -> end-card, which is reused as the plate canvas: select_by_prompt(23)->fill_area(24)=lower-third caption bar -> its output feeds select_by_prompt(25)->fill_area(26)=callout badge. Finally asset_copy_assets(27) consumes all ten produced deliverables plus the ingested caption sheet into one handoff folder, and asset_share_link(28) consumes that folder -> client share link. Every post-ingest step sets inputs_from to a prior step's named output (or the finalized ingest set for a legitimate sub-chain root); the deliverable package emerges from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 28 connector calls, 20 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four connector groups (asset/storage/share, video/audio [A], photo/raster grade [C], vector + solid-fill plates + font advisory) with two parallel sub-chains that converge into one Premiere-handoff package — well past the >=12 calls / >=3 groups / >=6 distinct-tool flagship bar, and the connector-doable interpretation of a real UGC/VSL/mashup paid-social ad job that would take a freelancer hours. Difficulty comes from (a) honoring the [A] async vs [C] headless split correctly — the seven video/audio deliverables (cleaned UGC audio, ~35s mashup master, two hook-variant reels, and the 16:9 / 1:1 / two 9:16 reframes) are async-widget/product-doable, while the durable creative spine (7-link thumbnail grade with a real select->masked-vibrance subject pop, logo SVG, color-overlay end-card, two selection->solid-fill plates, font recommendation, packaging + share) is fully headless-confirmed; (b) respecting the hard limits while still satisfying the brief's 'on-brand text / captions / callout graphics' intent — the connector does NOT composite text/graphics/captions onto video, so the captions ship as a supplied cue-sheet DATA asset and the lower-third + callout + end-card ship as standalone graphic PLATES the editor lays on the timeline; the multi-hook variants are SEPARATE Quick Cut passes (distinct target_duration + user_prompt), NOT video-trim-to-timestamp; video_resize is same-length reframe only; image_fill_area is solid-color only; font_recommend is advisory (no letterform design); video_metadata is a read-only probe; no generative fill, no text-to-image / from-scratch design, no AI object removal, no upscaling; (c) keeping every post-ingest step strictly output->input chained across a multi-creator mashup, three hook variants, three aspect ratios, a graded cover, and a plate set into a single coherent editable-asset package; and (d) the health_wellness compliance overlay (structure/function copy only, no disease/medical claims) threaded through the hooks, captions, and brand-kit steering.

reverify.banned_capability_check: Confirmed NONE of the banned capabilities are used. No generative fill / generative-expand of new content; no canvas extension/padding (image_crop_and_resize at step 20 only crops/resizes the existing hero frame to 1080x1920, output <= source dims, it does not enlarge the canvas); no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo/motion-graphics design; no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design (font_recommend only recommends an existing typeface). image_fill_area is solid-color fill only, painting a region onto the existing OPAQUE end-card canvas — the lower-third + callout plates are opaque swatch/position references, NOT transparent cutouts (headless selection-fill cannot produce alpha transparency, so the plate outputs are honestly specified as opaque PNG frames). image_select_by_prompt + masked image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation is a real selective edit (subject pop). image_vectorize traces the whole supplied flat logo raster -> SVG (no OCR / region-extraction claim). video_create_quick_cut is visual-engagement AI cutting driven by free-text hook/soundbite/summary steering (NOT timestamp trimming and NOT speech-content transcription) — the three hook variants are independent tighter passes via distinct target_duration + user_prompt, not exact-timestamp trims of the master. video_resize is same-length reframe (aspect only, no trimming) to 16:9 / 1:1 / 9:16. media_enhance_speech is voice-only UGC-audio cleanup (isolates a cleaned speech track; not music mastering/loudness). media_summarize is content/flow summarization of the talking heads + b-roll (no beat/BPM/tempo claim). video_metadata is a read-only probe. No text, captions, or graphics are composited onto video — captions ship as a supplied DATA cue sheet and all overlays ship as standalone PNG/SVG plates the editor burns during the manual finish; the logo lockup placement is named as the single human-finish composite step. asset_copy_assets / asset_share_link only package and share CC assets the chain itself produced (plus the supplied caption sheet).
reverify.notes: All 28 steps are real Adobe-connector actions drawn only from the approved connector set (asset_create_folders, asset_add_file, video_metadata, media_enhance_speech, media_summarize, font_recommend, video_create_quick_cut, video_resize, image_apply_auto_tone, image_adjust_color_temperature, image_adjust_hsl, image_select_by_prompt, image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation, image_adjust_light_portions, image_crop_and_resize, image_vectorize, image_apply_color_overlay, image_fill_area, asset_copy_assets, asset_share_link), each server=='adobe', with a coherent output->input chain. No init/finalize-upload or Firefly-board tools are used (ingest is the single asset_add_file; packaging is asset_copy_assets + asset_share_link). Every video/audio step (3,4,5,7,8,9,10,11,12,13) is honestly tagged — media/quick-cut/resize as [A] async-widget (product-doable, not headless-retrievable) and video_metadata as a [C] read; every still/plate/asset step is [C] headless-confirmed. Counts verified: connector_workflow has 28 steps -> tool_call_count=28; video_create_quick_cut appears 3x, video_resize 4x, image_select_by_prompt 3x, image_fill_area 2x but each counts once, giving 20 distinct adobe-server tools = distinct_adobe_tools. Slot parity: category=video_edit, slot_id=M07 (next free in the M video group after M01-M06), output_modality=video, workflow_nature=edit (transforms supplied UGC footage/assets into edited deliverables), vertical=health_wellness. Grounded in the real PeoplePerHour 'Video Ads for DTC Supplement Brand (UGC / VSL / Mashup)' brief with a fictional IP-clean brand (NOURA daily greens) and a realistic supplement-advertising compliance constraint (structure/function claims only).
